advantages: Deterministic installs across machines, CI and time.
Pins transitive dependencies, not just direct ones.
Hashes provide integrity and supply-chain protection.
Lock diffs make dependency changes reviewable.
disadvantages: Requires discipline: re-lock and commit on every change.
Stale locks block intended upgrades until refreshed.
Large lockfiles can be noisy in diffs.
Platform-specific locks may need per-OS variants (e.g. conda).
